name: get_available_slots
description: Look up real appointment slots for ONE day. Call this tool every time you need slots for a specific day or period — one call per day_query. NEVER invent times. day_query: 'tomorrow', 'day after tomorrow', or 'two days after tomorrow'. time_period: 'morning', 'afternoon', 'evening', or 'any' (only with time_preference). time_preference: only after the customer rejects the first two slots — pass their time frame verbatim; leave empty on the first morning/afternoon/evening offer. Read spoken_offer to the customer (includes weekday + date + two times, e.g. 'Monday June 15 — 8 AM or 10 AM'). If has_slots is false but has_any_slots is true, that period is empty — try another period on the same day or ask a custom time_preference. If has_any_slots is false, use next_day_query for the next tool call (max 3 days). If scheduling_window_exhausted is true, stop offering slots and use the handoff script. For book_slot, use the exact start from the chosen slot in slots.

query_params_schema.description: Day in the customer's words. Start with 'tomorrow'. When has_any_slots is false, call again with next_day_query from the previous response ('day after tomorrow', then 'two days after tomorrow'). Do not go beyond three days from today.
morning, afternoon, or evening when the customer picks a block. Use 'any' only when calling with a custom time_preference.

Customer's preferred time frame AFTER they reject the first two slots. Pass their exact wording: 'around 2', 'between 10 and 12', 'after 3 PM', 'before noon', 'early afternoon', etc. Leave empty when first offering morning/afternoon/evening slots.
